bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages, I target my maximum-utility bid and avoid conceding before seeing their priorities. Since time pressure is zero, there is no need to trade away value yet, especially on the high-weight issues C and D.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-max utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my opening offer. The opponent’s offer of all valueA is extremely poor for my preferences despite their fairness language, so I do not treat the message as meaningful evidence of a balanced deal.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA offer, which is very poor for me and shows no numerical concession despite cooperative-sounding messages, so I will hold firm on my high-weight issues C and D.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility offer for myself while continuing a small concession on the lowest-weight issueA. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA bid, which is extremely poor for me and shows no numerical concession despite their “win-win” messages, so I will not move on my high-weight issues C and D or on B yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.83 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering my worst values on every issue. Their messages claim fairness, but the offer history shows a firm all-valueA position, so I am only conceding on my lowest-weight issueA while holding my high-weight issues C and D at valueF.

I am targeting a high-utility bid by conceding only on my lowest-weight issue, issueA, while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D. Time is still early at t=0.10, and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueA despite cooperative messages, so I should not make large concessions yet.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is early in the negotiation, but I am making a measured concession from my last offer to avoid stalling. The opponent has mostly held firm at valueA, with only a small concession on issueC to valueB, so I will reciprocate by conceding on my lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.74 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held at valueA across issues, with only a small move to issueC:valueB, so I am making a modest non-repeated concession on issueC while protecting high-weight issueD and keeping issueB at a strong value.

I am targeting a still-strong early-game utility around 0.73, since time is only 0.16 and the opponent has made only a small concession on issueC while otherwise holding firm at valueA. Their messages claim fairness, but the actual offers remain very unfavorable to me, so I will make a modest concession without giving up the high-weight issues too far. I avoid repeating my previous bid by slightly relaxing issueD while keeping issueC and issueB relatively favorable.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the mid-0.6s because it is early in the negotiation at t=0.18, so there is no need to collapse toward the opponent’s low offer. The opponent has only made a small real concession on issueC while holding issueA, issueB, and issueD at valueA, so I am acknowledging their movement on issueC but continuing to protect high-weight issueD and issueC as much as possible.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the mid-0.6 range because we are only at t=0.20, so there is no need to collapse toward the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent’s bids show real movement only on issueC, while their “fair/balanced” messages are cheap talk, so I will acknowledge their issueC movement while making only a small concession on issueB and holding issueD relatively firm.

I am targeting a utility around the mid-0.64 range, since it is still early enough to hold firm on high-weight issueC and issueD while making small movement on lower-weight issueB. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and their messages overstate fairness, but they have shown some movement on issueC and issueA, so I am making a modest non-repeated counter rather than a large concession.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69, which is still favorable this early at t=0.24 but shows continued movement from my earlier high-utility demands. The opponent’s actual offers show flexibility mainly on issueC and very little on issueB/issueD, so I am matching their latest issueC movement while conceding on lower-weight issueA and moderately on issueB, but holding issueD high because it is one of my most important issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70 by combining concessions the opponent has already signaled separately: issueA=valueB, issueB=valueB, and issueC=valueC, while holding issueD=valueE because issueD is highly important to me. At t=0.26 there is still no need to make a large concession, and the opponent’s repeated “fair/win-win” messages are less informative than their actual slow movement on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68–0.70, keeping strong positions on the high-importance issues C and D while conceding more on low-importance issueA and moderately on issueB. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I do not need to match the opponent’s low-value offers; their messages claim fairness, but the offer history shows only limited concessions, with the latest useful movement on issueD.

I am targeting roughly the same strong utility as my prior bid while making a low-cost adjustment on issueB, where switching from valueC to valueD slightly improves my utility and matches the opponent’s latest movement. At t=0.30 there is still time, so I should not concede heavily on the high-weight issues C and D; the opponent’s messages claim balance, but their offers remain low for me and only show limited concessions.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.64 while making a meaningful concession from my prior bid on issueD, one of the issues where the opponent has just shown movement. At t=0.32 there is time left, so I should not collapse to their low offers, but their recent bids suggest they may be probing concessions on issueD and issueB while holding issueA low. Their messages claim balance, but the actual offers remain poor for me, so I am conceding selectively while protecting value on issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.65, since we are still before the midpoint and the opponent’s offers remain quite low for me despite their “win-win” language. Their actual bids show scattered concessions on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, so I am combining concessions they have separately signaled while holding reasonably firm on my high-weight issues C and D.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64, which is a moderate concession from my earlier offers but still protects my high-weight issues C and D. At t=0.36 there is time pressure to move, but not enough to accept the opponent’s currently low-value package; their offers show some movement on B, C, and D despite the messages overstating fairness. I am matching their recent movement on issueB while keeping issueC and issueD at values they have previously signaled some willingness to consider.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.58, conceding further on the lower/moderate-weight issues while still protecting high-weight issueC and issueD. The opponent’s offers have improved only slowly and remain low for me, but they have signaled some willingness on issueC and once on issueD, so I am matching their latest low positions on issueA/issueB while holding a plausible compromise on issueC/issueD. The messages claim fairness, but the numbers show limited concessions, so I am not rushing too far before the late phase.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.54, conceding further from my prior position because we are entering the mid-game but not yet near the deadline. The opponent’s messages claim balanced movement, but their actual offers remain low for me; still, they have separately shown willingness to move on issueC and issueD, so I am combining those signals while preserving some value on the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.54, keeping value on the high-weight issueC while conceding issueA and moving issueD to the opponent’s last signaled level. The opponent has gradually improved from all valueA offers and most recently offered issueC:valueD and issueD:valueC, so I am building around that real movement while adding a smaller ask on issueB that they have previously shown some willingness to move on; their “fair/win-win” messages are treated as non-binding cheap talk.

I am targeting a mid-game utility just above 0.51, conceding substantially from my early high-utility bids while still staying above the opponent’s latest offer value to me. The opponent has slowly improved from all valueA to offering valueC on issueC and valueB/C on issueD, but their messages overstate fairness, so I’m relying on the actual bids; I’m conceding issueB and issueA while asking for issueC/valueC and issueD/valueC, both of which are within their recent movement range.
